Name: Aaricetri
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Aquilae (The Star Wars)
Description: Aquilae was one of the first planet-names seen in the early drafts of Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope, dating back to the first story treatment. In his story notes for the film, when discussing the theme, Lucas stated that the planet's inspiration was the communist country North Vietnam, which he claimed was a "small, independent country."[1] In the third draft, Ben Kenobi still referenced Aquilae, along with the pl

Name: Arthézia
Description: Arthézia was a planet and the homeworld of Professor Adazian Loh'Khar. His family lived on the planet while he worked for the Flight Unlimited Group, an Imperial-aligned corporation. The corporation threatened to injure Loh'Khar's family if he stopped working for them.[1]

Name: Assorhan
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Chryselia
Description: Chryselia was a planet from which the pilot Dinia Olsen originated.[1]

Name: Cornwall
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Cynestra
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Dashaad
Description: The planet of Dashaad was initially planned to appear in Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic 3, a game set in the Old Republic era with John Stafford as the lead designer of the game as well as Molly Denmark and Cory Allemeier as two of the artists. The planet was created specifically for the game. Concept art for the game shows a ship resembling the Sith fighter known as the Dashaad fighter. The game was schedule

Name: Disney Playground Planet
Description: Disney Playground Planet was a leisure destination planet in the galaxy. Visitors typically went to Disney Parking Planet first.[1]

Name: Galtor VI
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Gathol
Description: Gathol was a planet.[1]

Name: Granicus
Description: Prior to 20 BBY, C-3PO served as one of 1,200 interpreter droids at the sub-embassies in the Granicus system, assigned to a "third liaison officer" He served as an interpreter for 37 years before he was eventually redesignated a protocol droid and sent to the Outlands to help welcome new systems into the Old Republic.

Name: Kinzor
Description: The nearest system was the Dagmar system.[1]

Name: Kiss
Description: Kiss was a planet. It was the homeworld of the entertainer Lily Langtree.

Name: Klaptun IV
Description: Klaptun IV was a planet. A group of mercenaries was once dispatched to the planet to destroy a factory there while in Tyrias, mercenaries were dispatched to kill its governor.[1]

Name: KX-112
Description: KX-112 was a small planet that orbited a star away from any of the major commercial routes. It was associated with the Galactic Empire and the trafficking of the narcotic Blue. During the Galactic Civil War, a group of Rebel investigators went under cover to the planet Sanod to find the source of the drug trafficking. Their investigation led them to this small planet.

Name: La'drach
Description: The content of this article was cut.

Name: Lop's homeworld
Description: A planet was the original homeworld of the Lepi Lop. After Lop escaped being enslaved by the Galactic Empire, she was adopted by the Yasaburō clan and came to consider the planet Tao to be her homeworld. Lop later mentioned the planet to her sister Ochō.[1]

Name: Marriten
Description: This article is non-canon within the canon continuity.

Name: Mithra
Description: Mithra appeared in the 1989 RPG adventure, "Prélude...", published in the fifteenth issue of the French magazine Chroniques d'Outre Monde. Therein, the planet was stated to be thirty standard days from the current position of the Rebel fleet and fourteen days travel from Gilkahande according to the Astrogation gazetteer included with the adventure.[1] The planet shares its name with the realm of Mithra, Jim Henson's

Name: Monsolar
Description: Monsolar was a planet located in the Alzoc system. A Imperial repair depot was located over Monsolor.[1]

Name: Nayeon
Description: This article is non-canon within the canon continuity.

Name: Ogana Major
Description: Ogana Major, or Ogana, was a huge blue Planet in the Kessil system. It was orbited by five moons, including the Fourth Moon of Ogana.[1]

Name: Ohnahmi
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Ophuchi
Description: Ophuchi or Ophichi was the name of a planet in the Ophuchi system, the homeworld of Mace Windy, a Jedi-Bendu in the Journal of the Whills, Part I.[1]

Name: Patagonia
Description: Patagonia was mentioned in "An Apology," a short story by Timothy Zahn that was released on April 1, 2012 as an April Fool's Day joke.

Name: Peekoine
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Pelicd
Description: Pelicd was the third planet in the Trebodar system. It was a barren rock wasteland which had one moon.

Name: Polarca VII
Description: Polarca VII was a small planet located in a neighboring star system – approximately two hours hyperspace travel – to Foerost. 10,000 kilometers above the surface, Station 14 geosynchronously orbited the world.[1]

Name: Rakanal
Description: Rakanal was a planet located in the galaxy. The Rakanal Run took place near Rakanal.[1]

Name: Rei'izu
Description: Rei'izu (pronunciation (help·info)) was the homeworld of the feudal Empire. Its capital city was Yojou.[1]

Name: Ristal
Description: Ristal was created by Jean Balczesak for the ambiguously canon roleplaying scenario L'écume de l'espace which appeared in the French magazine Casus Belli 44.[1]

Name: Roov
Description: Roov was a planet located in the galaxy.[1]

Name: Sanod
Description: Sanod was a planet beyond the borders of the Galactic Empire that was a haven for trafficking of the narcotic Blue. During the Galactic Civil War, a group of Rebel investigators went under cover on Sanod to find the source of the drug trafficking. As part of their cover, they were given an internship in administrative techniques at the spaceport in Istix, the planet's capital and only city.

Name: Shingra
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Solstice V
Description: Solstice V was a planet in the Al-ghenis system.

Name: Soum
Description: Soum was only mentioned in Les démons de feu, a roleplaying adventure published in the 25th issue of French gaming magazine Graal.[1]

Name: Séléann
Description: Séléann was a desert planet that was the homeworld to the predatory Tian-Ki. There, a farmer named Corran hunted the creatures with his blaster rifle.

Name: Talman III
Description: Talman III was a hot desert planet known for its mining activities and relative lawlessness. Some time during the Galactic Civil War, the planet contained a warehouse that supplied the Imperial logistics station in the Rama system with air filters as well as a spaceport and a nightclub.[1]

Name: Tyrias
Description: Tyrias was a planet. A group of mercenaries was once dispatched to the planet to kill its governor while in Klaptun IV, a group of mercenaries were dispatched to destroy a factory in the planet.[1]

Name: Unidentified orange planet
Description: An orange planet was located in the Earth system. Comparable in size to a gas giant, it was the second-closest planet to Earth.

Name: Unidentified planet (Mitaka)
Description: Shortly after, T0-B1 left Mitaka's residence and found the late Jedi's grave, putting fragments of the late Jedi's lightsaber within it. The droid resumed researching how to create life and eventually succeeded, bringing grass and rain to his homeworld. While admiring the rain with the other droids, the Inquisitor who had killed Mitaka arrived in a TIE/ad v1 to engage T0-B1, who he believed was a Jedi. The droid then

Name: Unidentified planet (Okinaa)
Description: A planet was the location of a village that the Sith Okinaa lived in. Okinaa lived on the planet for at least ten years, and was one of the last remaining members of the village along with their droid, R5-D56. Okinaa was killed by the Ronin in a duel that began in a holy place on the planet.[1]

Name: Unidentified red planet
Description: A red planet was located in the Earth system. It was the closest planet to Earth.

Name: Unidentified yellow ringed planet
Description: A yellow planet with a planetary ring was located in the Earth system. It was the farthest known planet to Earth.

Name: Utapau (Kessil system)
Description: Utapau was a planet in the Kessil system.

Name: Vald
Description: Vald was a planet which Grand Moff Lotter was once responsible for enslaving. However, after the defeat of the Galactic Empire by the New Republic, he ordered the genocide of the local populace. When the New Republic heard word of this, they sent a group of agents to track him down.[1]

Name: Waltar
Description: Waltar was a planet on which Dwitzel worked in a brothel before rejoining the Republic Army.[1]

Name: Yaloris
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Yoshiro
Description: Yoshiro was a desolate planet. Mace Windy and Chuiee Two Thorpe once went on a mission their from the Chairman of the Alliance.[1]

Name: Arges
Description: Arges was a planet that was believed to be the homeworld of the arges frog, a type of frog that had come to inhabit the forests and deserts of several other worlds, including Nevarro, Ossus, and Arvala-7, by the New Republic Era. Galactic Society of Creature Enthusiasts Director of Creature Cartography Ardis San Tekka mentioned Arges when writing about arges frogs in her journal[1] at some point after the Battle of E

Name: Chita's homeworld
Description: A planet was the homeworld of the human smuggler Chita.[1] At some point between 19 BBY and 0 ABY,[2] the Galactic Empire arrived to the world and, according to Chita, "made a mess" of the planet and her family, viewing the event as awful. Later,[1] at some point between 2 BBY and 0 ABY,[3] Chita told the former Jedi Gleenu of her homeworld when talking of the planet Zenarita's struggle under Imperial rule, mentionin

Name: Cora Vessora's homeworld
Description: A planet was the homeworld of Cora Vessora, a witch who refused to join the Jedi or the Sith and vowed revenge against the two parties when the Sith killed her loved ones and the Jedi did nothing to stop it. The planet, as well as Vessora, later appeared in the opera piece The Cantata of Cora Vessora, which chronicled Vessora's life.[1]

Name: Corrida
Description: Corrida was a planet that the treasure hunter Vette visited sometime during the Cold War. She nearly died while exploring a cave there.[1]

Name: Dezon's homeworld
Description: Pit Lieutenant Dezon was conscripted from his home planet.[1]

Name: Duzay
Description: Duzay was a world in the galaxy.[1] In 1 ABY,[2] a group of tourists from Duzay sojourned at the Lake Retreat Hotel during their stay on Naboo. An employee of the hotel named Rovim Minnoni recruited a spacer to rescue these tourists when they got lost during a walk in the wilderness.[1]

Name: Dymano
Description: Dymano was a planet on which a circus was held. The planet was habitable for humans.[1] At some point between 19 BBY and 2 BBY,[2] the former Sith Lord Maul—disguised as the Sith Lord Darth Vader—took a squad of Imperial stormtroopers to the planet. On Dynamo, the squad stole string lights and red lipstick from the circus while Maul held a clown with pie on its face in the air using the Force. One of the stormtrooper

Name: Everon
Description: Before the High Republic Era,[2] the Evereni put their trust in their stormy homeworld of Everon in an attempt to live in harmony with nature; however, the Great Storm came, in the form of a vortex storm that rose from the planet's core bringing strong winds and lightning, destroying the native Evereni's civilization.[3]

Name: Exo
Description: Exo was a legendary planet that may or may not have existed. The philosopher Deamos Na-Coth believed in the existence of the planet, and founded a series of expeditions known as The Cabal to search beyond the Outer Rim Territories to find the planet.

Name: Garth Ezzar's homeworld
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Sturg Ganna's homeworld
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Geldoll
Description: Geldoll was a planet in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the Sith Empire and the Galactic Republic, Geldoll banned all Hutt starships and representatives from the planet in order to fight smuggling.[1]

Name: Gen'Dai homeworld
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: The Graveyard of the Dragons
Description: The Graveyard of the Dragons was the putative planet where all Duinuogwuin traveled to die. The Graveyard of the Dragons of Xuaquarres was believed to be The Graveyard of the Dragons.

Name: J'doytzin III
Description: J'doytzin III was a planet in the galaxy. During the Clone Wars, Chancellor Palpatine negotiated with their Shahmistra. The meeting was so tedious in Palpatine's eyes that he promised himself J'doytzin III would be among the first worlds to feel the wrath of the Galactic Empire when it rose.[1]

Name: Jod Na Nawood's homeworld
Description: A planet was the homeworld of Jod Na Nawood, a human male pirate by the New Republic Era. The planet was known for being covered in fog, or "ashy dust" like Na Nawood described. Upon landing on At Achrann with the Onyx Cinder crew and their droid, Na Nawood commented that At Achrann's fog felt similar to his homeworld's.[1]

Name: Koorivar homeworld
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Rahm Kota's homeworld
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Leox Gyasi's homeworld
Description: The human pilot Leox Gyasi was raised on a peaceful world, so he had little knowledge of the concept of a draft.[1]

Name: Llewebum
Description: Jason Fry, co-author of The Essential Atlas, indicated in March 2023 that Llewebum was validated for a future update of The Essential Atlas Online Companion.[1]

Name: Logan Erstar's homeworld
Description: A world at one point by the time of the Galactic Civil War between the Galactic Empire and the Rebel Alliance had royal families and governing ministers. Logan Erstar, Baron of Filby, was born there.[1]

Name: Lupr'or homeworld
Description: Many years before the Battle of Starkiller Base, the Lupr'or settled on the planet Luprora after they escaped a planet that had been spoiled by technology. After realizing that technology would be the end of them if they did not change, the Lupr'or buried and destroyed the technology that had brought them to Luprora.[1]

Name: Maateen
Description: Maateen was a planet in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the Sith Empire and the Galactic Republic, the strong repression of Maateen authorities forced the Exchange to recruit new personnel to assure weapons shipment delivery to the planet.[1]

Name: Menrec
Description: This article is non-canon within the canon continuity.

Name: Nagi
Description: Nagi was first indirectly mentioned in the current Star Wars canon in the second issue of the 2025 comic series Star Wars. The issue was written by Alex Segura and published by Marvel Comics[2] on June 11, 2025.[5] It was later identified in the series' fifth issue, also written by Segura and published by Marvel[4] on September 17 of the same year.[6]

Name: Pedorian
Description: Pedorian was a planet that experienced a conflict known as the Pedorian Civil War during the Cold War between the Galactic Republic and the reconstituted Sith Empire. The Hutt Cartel supplied weapons to both factions in the war.[1]

Name: Promencious Four
Description: Promencious Four was mentioned in Star Wars Legends in Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Eternal Throne, the sixth digital expansion of the 2011 BioWare game Star Wars: The Old Republic.[1] Promencius Four originated in the 2015 canon novel Battlefront: Twilight Company, written by Alexander Freed.[2]

Name: Trebold Raynor's homeworld
Description: The homeworld of Trebold Raynor was the rumored resting place of the Platinum Pickaxe. Gan Alno, head of Raynor Mining Enterprises during the Galactic War, hired treasure hunters to search Raynor's homeworld for the pickaxe.

Name: Riggs Ashar's homeworld
Description: A planet was the homeworld of Riggs Ashar, who worked for the planet's militia by guarding the landing area at a spaceport. Near the spaceport, there was a bar that Ashar frequented.

Name: Rumil
Description: Rumil was a planet in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the reconstituted Sith Empire and the Galactic Republic, personal blasters were banned on Rumil. The Exchange sensed an opportunity, and wished to hire someone to deliver black market weapons to Rumil.[1]

Name: Safe Home
Description: Safe Home was the name given to an uncharted planet by a group of independent scouts who discovered it during the Galactic Civil War. The scouts, members of the old Scout Service, kept the existence of the planet secret and retired on this world to escape the horrors of war. They were supplied by a group of pirates who had managed to convince them that the Rebel Alliance had been defeated by the Galactic Empire.

Name: Sagamore
Description: Sagamore was a planet that the Kotabi bond-twins Ceret and Terec,[1] who served as Jedi Knights of the Jedi Order during the High Republic Era,[2] originated from.[1] Alongside the planet Kotab,[3] Sagamore was considered the twin's homeworld.[4] In 231 BBY,[5] while fighting the Nihil pirates on the Outer Rim planet Valo, the Tholothian[6] Jedi Knight[2] Indeera Stokes mentally recalled having heard of Kotabi twins

Name: Sennoa
Description: Sennoa was a planet in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the reconstituted Sith Empire and the Galactic Republic, the freelance assassin Tertra Essa was arrested by authorities on Sennoa.[1]

Name: Skeressa
Description: Skeressa was a planet in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the Sith Empire and the Galactic Republic, Skeedo the Hutt hired individuals to hijack arms shipments to the local militia of Skeressa.[1]

Name: Snee homeworld
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Suboreen
Description: Suboreen first appeared in the third issue of the canon comic book series Star Wars, published by Marvel Comics on July 16, 2025.[1]

Name: Matt Talon's homeworld
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Teklos
Description: Jason Fry, co-author of The Essential Atlas, indicated in March 2023 that Teklos was validated for a future update of The Essential Atlas Online Companion.[1]

Name: Temesher
Description: Temesher was a planet in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the reconstituted Sith Empire and the Galactic Republic, a large amount of cortosis was discovered on Temesher underneath a graveyard used by the natives' warriors.[1]

Name: Tormin
Description: Tormin was a planet in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the Galactic Republic and the Sith Empire, Colonel Frellder went missing there, and investigation found that Frellder's own men had killed him due to his harsh style of command.[1]

Name: Tulix
Description: This article is non-canon within the canon continuity.

Name: U-One
Description: U-One was a legendary planet, populated solely by droids.

Name: Unidentified 26-hour planet
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified backwater planet
Description: A backwater planet hosted a vital Imperial communications hub on Sunfall Peak. A resistance group fought the Imperial presence there before joining with the Rebel Alliance to stage a raid on the hub.[1]

Name: Unidentified Corellian colony
Description: This Corellian colony was the homeworld of the criminal Sufar. In 2 BBY, the colony exiled him for a series of murders he committed.

Name: Unidentified craggy planet
Description: A craggy planet was located somewhere in the galaxy between the planets Arbooine and Eriadu. Near the end of the Clone Wars, the Jedi Knight Suljo Warde stopped at the planet and left his holocron on the planet's surface.[1]

Name: Unidentified green planet
Description: A green planet was located in the galaxy. During the Galactic Civil War, a small battle took place between the Galactic Empire and an automated CR90 corvette over the planet.[1]

Name: Unidentified green-skied planet
Description: The planet first appeared in "Incognito," a short story by author John Jackson Miller and illustrated by Chris Scalf, which was published in Star Wars Insider 143 in 2013. The story served as a tie-in to Jackson's novel Kenobi, which was released later that year.

Name: Unidentified ice planet (Hailstorm Brotherhood)
Description: This ice planet was the original home of the fanatical subfaction of the White Maw pirates known as the Hailstorm Brotherhood before relocating to the planet Hoth.[1]

Name: Unidentified mountainous planet (Spectres)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified Nikto colony world
Description: A colony world of the Nikto species was ruled by a grand chieftain during the Cold War between the Galactic Republic and the reconstituted Sith Empire, and the chieftain allowed his people to freely prey on passing starships until intimidated by offworlders.[1]

Name: Unidentified planet (A-rack)
Description: A planet, which was liberated by Empire of the Hand Grand Admiral Thrawn, was the origin point of the A-rack rolling vehicles the Grand Admiral used during a battle against alien warlord Nuso Esva on the[1] Unknown Regions[2] planet Quethold[1] in 8 ABY.[3] Imperial Stromma contingent stormtrooper Lhagva remembered where the A-racks originated during the conflict.[1]

Name: Unidentified planet (blue-skinned race)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified planet (Canderous Ordo)
Description: A planet was attacked by the Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders during the Mandalorian Wars. Canderous Ordo recalled the battle to Revan during the Jedi Civil War describing it as a "placid" world.

Name: Unidentified planet (Darth Vader)
Description: A remote uninhabited planet was located beyond the Outer Rim Territories. Around the Galactic Civil War, Darth Vader overheard Graballa the Hutt informing Vader that he had captured Rowan Freemaker at the former Freemaker garage. Later M-OC questioned Vader about the location of Rowan, Vader tricked him and sent him to this remote planet.[1]

Name: Unidentified planet (Dragon Void Run)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified planet (Exim Panshard)
Description: A planet was ruled by the Sith Lord Viceroy Exim Panshard prior to his death.[1] By 21 ABY,[2] the planet had become long forgotten and the viceroy's tyrannical reign of the planet was told in legends. After traveling to the asteroid known as Kiza's world, Jedi Master Luke Skywalker came across the mask of Viceroy Exim Panshard that was in the possession of Kiza. Panshard's spirit, which lived on in the mask, showed

Name: Unidentified planet (Fermata Cage)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified planet (Gell Mattar)
Description: A planet was the location of the city of Gell Mattar during the Great Galactic War, and it was the site of a battle in 3666 BBY.

Name: Unidentified planet (Jedi temple)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified planet (Klikklak)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified planet (Nes Ukul)
Description: The Jedi Padawan Nes Ukul died on a planet whose name he did not know, defending a species whose language he could not speak. Nes Ukul's master later called the sacrifice noble. The antiques dealer Antron Bach remembered the story of Ukul's sacrifice on the planet while trying to decide if he should potentially sacrifice himself so that the Corellian Resistance member Miru Nadrinakar could escape from the Galactic Em

Name: Unidentified planet (Nuso Esva's commanders)
Description: In 0 ABY,[2] Storm-hair[3] Warlord Nuso Esva traveled aboard his Kazellis-class light freighter Salaban's Hope to an unknown and unremarkable terrestrial planet that orbited an unremarkable sun for more than five hours before returning to the Imperial II-class Star Destroyer Chimaera. Senior Captain Thrawn of the Galactic Empire speculated that the world was where Esva met with his commanders to finish their preparat

Name: Unidentified planet (old Hamma's place)
Description: A planet was the location to where Jyn Erso fled after her visit to Tamsye Prime went awry. Akshaya Ponta met Erso on that planet as Erso was looking to escape[1] around 5 BBY.[2]

Name: Unidentified planet (Outreachial)
Description: A planet was the site of the settlement of Outreachial, and another settlement which hosted a bazaar. The planet was home to lizardflies, who lived in cliffs above Outreachial.

Name: Unidentified planet (Poe Dameron)
Description: In 35 ABY,[2] during the war between the First Order and the Resistance, Commander Poe Dameron of the Resistance, having learned of the hiding spot sometime prior, guided his fellow Resistance members to the planet's rings in the starship Bestoon Legacy following a mission to the planet Pasaana. Shortly afterward, the Resistance members departed for the planet Kijimi,[1] but were followed by the starship of the dark

Name: Unidentified planet (Rhydonium heist)
Description: The planet appeared, though it was not identified, in "I Have Friends Everywhere," the fifth episode of the second season of the television series Star Wars: Andor released on Disney+[1] on April 29, 2025.[4]

Name: Unidentified planet (sabacc)
Description: A planet was home to a casino where Han Solo once cheated in a game of sabacc using funds for the Rebel Alliance. Solo's fellow gamblers discovered he was cheating, and chased him and Luke Skywalker off the planet without their money.[1]

Name: Unidentified planet (seven rings)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified planet (Skywalker and Tano)
Description: A planet was the location of a battle in the Clone Wars between Republic forces under Jedi General Anakin Skywalker and Jedi Commander Ahsoka Tano and enemy artillery.

Name: Unidentified planet (Tholme and Ventress)
Description: A planet was the site of a battle between the Galactic Republic and the Confederacy of Independent Systems early on in the Clone Wars. During the battle, Jedi Master Tholme fought the assassin Asajj Ventress, and died by her hand.[1]

Name: Unidentified planet (Unduli and Offee)
Description: A rocky planet existed, during the Clone Wars a battle took place between the Galactic Republic and the Confederacy of Independent Systems.[1]

Name: Unidentified planet (Vestara Khai)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified planet (Wookiees)
Description: According to a legend,[2] a long time before[1] 0 BBY,[3] a planet served as the home of the Wookiee species. However, they left the planet in favor of the world of Kashyyyk,[1] located in the Mid Rim's Mytaranor sector.[4]

Name: Unidentified planet (Younglings)
Description: This unidentified planet was a planet in the galaxy. During the Clone Wars, Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker took a Jedi Initiate Clan on a training mission to this planet. Members of the group included Jedi younglings Ammon, Cryle and Tryla.

Name: Unidentified red-skied planet
Description: The planet was inhabited by humanoid natives that were enslaved by the Confederacy of Independent Systems in 19 BBY.

Name: Unidentified sandy planet (Dragon Void Run)
Description: A sandy planet was visited by the smugglers Han Solo and Chewbacca during the Dragon Void Run.[1]

Name: Unidentified uninhabited refuge world
Description: The world was mentioned in the current Star Wars canon in the 2021 novel Thrawn Ascendancy: Lesser Evil, the final entry in Timothy Zahn's The Ascendancy Trilogy.[1] The mention of the world drew upon Zahn's Star Wars Legends continuity novel Outbound Flight, which was released in 2006 and detailed the fate of the Outbound Flight starship. In the novel, Chiss Syndic Mitth'ras'safis and Human Jedi Knight Lorana Jinzle

Name: Unidentified world (Orla Jareni)
Description: During her time as a Jedi Padawan Orla Jareni undertook a mission to a world known for its beautifully realistic scuplture. The worlds artists crafted statues that were so lifelike they seemed to have softness, texture, and spirit. Jareni recalled the statues when she looked upon the husked remains of fellow Jedi Knight Regald Coll after he had been killed aboard Starlight Beacon by a Nameless creature.[1]

Name: Unidentified world (Voegliss)
Description: At one point during the reign of the Galactic Empire, the Imperial scientist Devlor Samt and his team surveyed a supposedly uninhabited world. There, the team came under a ferocious attack by two members of a sentient species that was not native to the world.[1]

Name: Vahla homeworld
Description: The Vahla homeworld was the homeworld of the Vahla, a Force-sensitive, Near-Human species with ties to the dark side. The planet was believed to have been taken over by Vahl, a Force Adept who led her followers and was later deified by the cultists of the Ember of Vahl. The ancient Vahla were regarded as twisted and decadent, and several thousand years before the Sith–Imperial War, the cult was decimated by the Jedi

Name: Varovia
Description: Varovia was a planet located in the galaxy that was the homeworld of the Varovian species. Jedi Master Atticus Cale, his Padawan Jadia Brink, Golo and Tilli traveled to Varovia in search for the missing Jedi Master Barnabas Vim, but had no luck in discovering him on the planet. Instead they destroyed the Rod of Ages which helped stop the civil war the Varovians were having on their homeworld.[1]

Name: Coh Veshiv's homeworld
Description: A planet with plant life on it was the homeworld of the senator Coh Veshiv.

Name: Katiz Xemir's homeworld
Description: The Human Katiz Xemir settled on a remote colony planet and called it home. However, after Xemir refused to relocate offworld to make way for a mining facility on the planet, the Galactic Empire killed his family. That caused Xemir to hire himself out as a mercenary against Imperial targets.[1]

Name: Zilka home planet
Description: The insectoid Zilka sentient species originated from a planet. When the gangster Chulok analyzed two unconscious Zilka enforcers who had been incapacitated by the incognito Jedi Master Mace Windu, the Rodian Lok—one half of Chulok—noted that Windu had been familiar with Zilka anatomy, suggesting he had either been to their planet or had studied them.[1]

Name: Lahn
Description: This article contains information from an unlicensed Star Wars Legends source.

Name: Celadon
Description: The title of this article is a nickname, call sign, or alias.

Name: Dead Moon of Gothol
Description: The capitalization in this article's title is conjectural.

Name: Gelemeade's fourth moon
Description: The fourth moon of Gelemeade was first mentioned in BioWare's 2011 massively multiplayer online roleplaying game Star Wars: The Old Republic, in the Bioanalysis crew skill mission "Signs of Life".[1]

Name: Hyban
Description: Hyban was a moon located in the Calzag system. Competitors in a Starlight Run were required to navigate through the moon.[1]

Name: Senaru's homeworld
Description: The Arcona Senaru lived on a moon during the Cold War between the Galactic Republic and the Sith Empire. The moon's thriving ecosystem was threatened when a freighter crashed on the moon and spilled hrucium-5 onto the surface, so Senaru traveled to the planet Taris in order to study how that planet's wildlife had adapted to toxic environments in hopes of saving his world.

Name: Unidentified airless moon (Olly)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified isolated moon
Description: This moon was first mentioned in the 2014 novel Tarkin by author James Luceno. It did not receive a name.

Name: Unidentified moon (Ekiya)
Description: A terrestrial moon was the site of a battle between the Sith and the Jedi clans. Ekiya and other individuals forced to fight for the Sith were present on the moon.[1]

Name: Unidentified moon (Heinsnake Cult)
Description: A moon was the location of the Heinsnake Cult of dark side sorcerers. The moon's energy was utilized into a superlaser via the Basis.

Name: Unidentified moon (Ochi)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified moon (Siline's star system)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Useria's moon
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Veeros
Description: Veeros was a remote moon whose sole inhabitant and ruler was Atha Prime during the Republic Era. He was attended by an army of loyal droid attendants, including his majordomo Blue-Tark, and lived in a palace surrounded by bio-fields, which were renowned offworld and produced a annual crop yield. In secret, Prime also conducted dark experiments to create a horde of xerexi to use against what he percieved as the ills o

Name: Y'ness
Description: Y'ness was a moon located in the galaxy. Volcanic activity on the moon produced kimber stones.[1]

Name: Abrion system
Description: The Abrion system first appeared in the non-canon 2016 Disney XD animated television series LEGO Star Wars: The Freemaker Adventures. It was first identified as the location of the Wheel space station in the one-minute LEGO Star Wars video "Freemaker Salvage and Repair" on August 29, 2016.[1] While non-canon, the Freemaker Adventures was produced in conjunction with the Lucasfilm Story Group.[2]

Name: Calzag system
Description: This article is non-canon within the canon continuity.

Name: Hazell system
Description: The Hazell system was a star system where a rebel base was located.[1]

Name: Marriten system
Description: The Marriten system was a star system that contained the planet Marriten. After Durpin and Plumestriker gathered the Kyber crystal necklaces, they retreated to the Marriten system.[1]

Name: Phalan system
Description: The Phalan system was a star system where an Imperial space station was located.[1]

Name: Roonscal system
Description: This article is non-canon within the canon continuity.

Name: Amosu system
Description: The Amosu system was a star system in the galaxy that contained three planets, though only the largest planet Tamo'Akora was considered habitable. Major Gohl and a unit of First Order stormtroopers stopped in the Amosu system during a training tour, docking aboard the bulk freighter Loxodonta.[1]

Name: Bandorra system
Description: The Bandorra system was a star system. Imperial Military forces were present in the system during the Galactic Civil War.[1]

Name: Chommell system
Description: The Chommell system was a star system in the galaxy that saw a number of guerrilla attacks on Galactic Republic convoys by pirates during the Cold War. The pirates were eventually tracked to a number of bases in the sector and eliminated.[1]

Name: Cina system
Description: Boba Fett? Boba Fett? Where?

Name: Croynar sector alphanumeric designated star system
Description: The alphanumeric star system was located in the Croynar sector within the Trailing Sectors portion of the Inner Rim Territories. Situated around the coordinates M-14 on the Standard Galactic Grid,[1] it was cluttered with planetoids and debris.[2]

Name: Ekuda system
Description: The Ekuda system appeared in a simulator mission in the video game Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter. The game specified that simulator missions were simulations of historical battles.[1]

Name: Gallo system
Description: Boba Fett? Boba Fett? Where?

Name: Junquers system
Description: The Junquers system was first mentioned in Bill Slavicsek's 1989 Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game adventure, Otherspace. The system received a single mention in the bio for Imperial agent, EL-434, a cerillium composite motoball assassin droid captured by the Alliance, that was being held aboard the Celestial alongside Tolonda sector's Grand Moff Ravik and Imperial Outer Rim Territories Patrol Fleet commander, Bane Not

Name: Kellis system
Description: The Kellis system was located in the galaxy. During the Cold War between the reconstituted Sith Empire and the Galactic Republic, the system was infested with smugglers.[1] Around 3643 BBY,[2] Ungo the Hutt was looking to hire security to escort a shipment of theirs through the system.[1]

Name: Lamman system
Description: At one point during that conflict, a group of Force-sensitive individuals researching TK-575 in the Archive discovered the mission report.[1]

Name: Manali system
Description: The Manali system[1] was a star system located in the galaxy.[2]

Name: Reidi Artom II system
Description: The Reidi Artom II system was a system discovered by Reidi Artom. It was eventually renamed with a local or ancient name. The discovery of this system was not particularly profitable for its discoverer.[1]

Name: Trinith system
Description: The Trinith system was a star system located within the galaxy. This system was the original home of the Trinthan prowler, with the feline predator eventually being bred across the galaxy due to its prowess with hunting and tracking. The Galactic Republic would later ban the breeding of the species, and the system's native predator could be found across a number of worlds, including Quesh as a result of being release

Name: Tryth system
Description: Tryth and the Tryth system were created for Sanctuary: A Bad Batch Novel, written by Lamar Giles and published on August 5, 2025.[1] Giles derived the name of the planet from the Tri-City area in the US state of Virginia, where Giles grew up. Tryth's mercury refinery stations were a nod to the fact Giles' home city of Hopewell is a factory town.[2]

Name: Unidentified dead star system
Description: A star system was centered around a dwarf star in the galaxy until the star died sometime by the Republic Era. A near impossibility of science, the lingering dwarf sat in space at temperatures near absolute zero, creating gravity wells and other quantum fluctuations around it.[1] In 29 BBY,[2] Jedi Knight Obi-Wan Kenobi traveled to the system on assignment from the Jedi High Council to study the star, unknowingly car

Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified star system (Grysk observation post)
Description: A star system that was centered around a sun contained the Grysk observation post, a portable space station used by the Grysk species.[1]

Name: Unidentified star system (Leia Organa)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified star system (Marda Ro)
Description: A star system was located in the galaxy. After leaving[1] the Outer Rim[2] planet Dalna and taking control of the starship Gaze Electric following[1] the Night of Sorrow in 382 BBY,[3] the Evereni Marda Ro instructed the ship's navigation computer to chart a course to a location where she would not be found by others. In response, the computer chose the star system. As Ro headed toward the system, she mentally noted

Name: Unidentified star system (Mulako Corporation Primordial Water Quarry)
Description: A star system was located in the galaxy and contained an average–sized orange star. The star was orbited by several planets as well as the Mulako Corporation Primordial Water Quarry comet. The water on the Mulako Corporation Quarry originated in the formation of the star system.

Name: Unidentified star system (red dwarf)
Description: A star system existed in the galaxy, and contained a red dwarf. Rogue Squadron ambushed some bacta tankers there in 7 ABY.

Name: Unidentified star system (rescue of C-3PO)
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified star system (Watith)
Description: A system was the home of the Watith sentient species.[1] In 18 BBY,[3] Chiss Senior Captain Mitth'raw'nuruodo (Core name "Thrawn") arrived at the Zyzek system, where he inquired the established traders and nations if they knew the whereabouts of the Watith home and if there was any Watith present to deliver his "prisoners"—which he had none of—to. The leader of the Kilji Illumine, Generalirius Nakirre then attempted

Name: Worosta's star system
Description: A star system contained the planet Worosta and was aligned with the Galactic Republic. During the Clone Wars, it was invaded by the Confederacy of Independent Systems, and the Separatists' exploitation of the world continued under the rule of the Galactic Empire. The journalist Ricta Varsh visited Worosta long before the Clone Wars, and Taylo Mor grew up on Worosta.[1]

Name: Altiria
Description: The Altiria/Anarris system was a star system located in the unexplored reaches of the galaxy[1] that was home[2] to the Altiri species.[3]

Name: Aquilaean system
Description: In the rough draft of the script for A New Hope, the astrography of the Aquilaean system(s) is somewhat confused. The destruction of the Imperial Space Fortress is visible from Yavin.

Name: Engai system
Description: The Engai system was a star system that Jedi master Numoda was sent to by Jedi lord Hanrai to root out smugglers. The Jedi apprentice Yuehiro and other apprentices in Hanrai's Jedi clan later heard there was a complication, after which the clan's masters refused to speak about the situation. Yuehiro came to believe that Numoda was dead, although his bones were not returned to Hanrai's shrine as they should have been.

Name: Sice system
Description: The Sice system was a star system that contained the planet Sicemon, a grassy world that was home to a Rebel Alliance base.[1]

Name: Unidentified star system (Lah Zhima)
Description: A star system was located far from the Outer Rim Territories planet Hy Izlan. After the sabersmith Lah Zhima was kidnapped by Jedi Hunters on Hy Izlan, he was taken to the star system. The Sith acolyte Niizo later told Zhima's daughter Lah Kara that he had been taken to the system.[1]

Name: Unidentified star system (mine)
Description: A star system was the site of a mine owned by a lord of the Empire. At a point when the mine was new, the lord desired labor for the mine and sent his Jedi to recruit volunteers on a small Outer Rim moon he controlled that neighbored the star system. Due to the Jedi's recruitment methods, the moon suffered, and the bounty hunter Chie challenged the Jedi to a duel, in which she killed all of them. The storyteller know

Name: Adim mining colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Attichitcuk's camp
Description: Attichitcuk's camp was a settlement built by the Wookiee Attichitcuk on the moon Alaris Prime at some point between 39 BBY and 36 BBY, during his group's colonization attempt of the moon. The camp was built in a small forest clearing and only had four buildings. After Attichitcuk built the camp, it was found by his son, Chewbacca, and his friend Shoran with the help of Jedi Master Qui-Gon Jinn. At the same time, four

Name: Candorian colonies
Description: The Candorian colonies were colonies of the planet Candoria.[2] In 46 BBY,[1] Candoria and its colonies were depopulated by an outbreak of the Blue Shadow Virus.[2] The disease was later known as the Candorian plague. The plague seemed to end then, but was introduced to Dentaal by the Empire forty-six years later.[1]

Name: Chagrian colony
Description: A colony of the Chagrian species existed on the planet Mindor during the Great Galactic War between the Galactic Republic and the reconstituted Sith Empire. Founded sometime before the year 3680 BBY, the colony was the birthplace of Fideltin Rusk, a Chagrian who rebelled against the colony's extreme pacifism and enlisted in the Republic Military. However, the colonists' refusal to take action against those who oppres

Name: Chewbacca's camp
Description: Chewbacca's camp was built on the moon Alaris Prime between 39 and 36 BBY by Wookiee colonists. It was run from a central command center and initially contained buildings used for collecting resources, along with two power cores and a troop center. The camp was run by Chewbacca, the leader of the Wookiees on Alaris Prime, and was a short distance from a camp run by his ally Shoran. It was expanded to include a spacep

Name: Crescent Canyon Ortolan outpost
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Dohnia free colony
Description: The Dohnia free colony was a colony on Dohnia that existed around the time of the end of the Clone Wars. The Cerean Ruf-Asti-Viarffli was President of it.[1]

Name: Dreshdae
Description: Dreshdae was a settlement on the planet Korriban that existed before the Jedi Civil War.

Name: Esstran sector colony
Description: A colony in the Esstran sector had completely dropped out of contact during the Galactic War. An investigation revealed that a new type of spice had gripped the entire colony.[1]

Name: Gabredor III colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Galaxeed
Description: Galaxeed was a colony located in the Roon system.[1] A Galaxeed team competed against teams representing Umboo and Tawntoom in the Roon Colonial Games[2] in 15 BBY.[3]

Name: Jarbanov colony
Description: The Jarbanov colony was located on the barren planet Jarbanov at the outskirts of the junker systems. A major processing center that featured domes, it was home to a disassembly plant, a battery recycling plant, and a row of radioactive hazard vault in its outskirts.[1] In 5 ABY,[2] the Galactic Empire's 204th Imperial Fighter Wing conducted an operation at Jarbanov where one of its squadrons, Squadron Four, attacked

Name: Kalakar Six settlement
Description: A mining colony was established on the volcanic moon Kalakar Six in the early days of the reconstituted Sith Empire, but was wiped out by a volcanic eruption and preserved in ash. During the Cold War between the Galactic Republic and the Sith Empire, the colony was rediscovered, and the holojournal of the governor was even found intact.[1]

Name: Keshk lunar colony
Description: The Keshk lunar colony was a colony where the Keshk Corporation operated during the Imperial Era.[1] In 3 ABY,[2] wage disputes led to strikes on the colony which lasted for at least eight days. On the strikes' eighth day, the Keshk lunar colony and the strikes were mentioned in a news report listened to by Lady Domina Tagge—the leader of Tagge Corporation—which also added that the Keshk Corporation declined to comme

Name: Lake Brell colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Lifh sector colony
Description: A colony of Humans was founded in the Lifh sector in the era before the invention of lightspeed travel. It was later rediscovered during the Cold War by operatives of the Sith Empire, who hid the colony before Galactic Republic research vessels in the area could find it.[1]

Name: Meridian sector colony
Description: A colony in the Meridian sector was a established during the Galactic War. Ripe for black market trade, it enabled ambitious local businessmen who laid the groundwork for future trading in the Meridian sector.[1]

Name: Moonbender Colony
Description: Moonbender Colony, also known simply as Moonbender, was a colony located on a moon in the Outer Rim during the Republic Era. The colony was run by the Remainders, a group of former bounty hunters.[1] By sometime between around 40 BBY and 32 BBY,[2] the colony's population had dwindled to around one hundred—miners and vapor farmers, as well as their families. During that time, the Sith apprentice Darth Maul was sent t

Name: Ord Cantrell Duros colony
Description: A colony of Duros was established on the planet Ord Cantrell that eventually lost contact with the galaxy at large and reverted to simple technology, such as wooden sailing ships. During the Cold War between the Galactic Republic and the reconstituted Sith Empire, the existence of the long-dead colony was discovered after a sailing ship was dredged from the planet's oceans.[1]

Name: Ralchon
Description: Ralchon[1] was an Twi'lek colony[4] in the Ralchon system in the Gaulus sector of the Outer Rim Territories,[3] located east[5] of the Twi'lek homeworld of Ryloth.[6]

Name: Schenor research station
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Second Colonial Protectorate
Description: The Second Colonial Protectorate appears in the Imperial version of the planet Taris in the video game Star Wars: The Old Republic. It takes the place of the Junction, a class-restricted area for the Jedi Consular in the Republic version of Taris, and the Junction is moved to the Tularan Marsh.

Name: Seventh Duro Relocation Colony
Description: The seventh Duro Relocation Colony was an effort by the Duros to settle other planets. They settled Pii III but eventually abandoned the colony due to the species love of spacefaring.

Name: Shoran's camp
Description: Shoran's camp was a walled settlement on the moon Alaris Prime run by the Wookiee Shoran. The camp was built during the war fought between the Wookiee colonists of Alaris Prime and the Trade Federation between 39 BBY and 36 BBY. Shoran's camp featured a command center, a spaceport, several prefab shelters, and other buildings for gathering resources. A Jedi holocron was found within the camp's walls and was retrieved

Name: Silunes
Description: Silunes was mentioned in the 2015 Star Wars Roleplaying sourcebook Strongholds of Resistance, which was published by Fantasy Flight Games for use with the Star Wars: Age of Rebellion roleplaying game system.[1]

Name: Stend IV colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Tigon mining colony
Description: The Tigon mining colony was a mining settlement on the[2] Mid Rim[3] planet of Makeb. When the Hutt Cartel invaded Makeb[2] in 3638 BBY,[1] they began massive mining operations on the planet, which triggered severe groundquakes.[4] The fourth of these groundquakes leveled the entirety of the Tigon mining colony, and bodies were still being removed from the rubble of the colony by the fifth groundquake. During the fif

Name: Unidentified colony (Halanit)
Description: A colony existed on the planet Halanit. It was destroyed by the Thyferran Home Defense Corps in 7 ABY.

Name: Unidentified colony (hollinium)
Description: A group of travelers eventually came across information regarding the conglomerate's plan and set out as the only ones capable of stopping the forthcoming massacre.[1]

Name: Unidentified colony (Selcaron)
Description: A colony affiliated with the New Republic existed on Selcaron,[3] one of the moons of the gas giant Selaggis VI. In 7 ABY, Selcaron was attacked by Warlord Zsinj, who used his Super Star Destroyer, the Iron Fist, to obliterate the colony.[1]

Name: Unidentified Geonosian colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified Imperial colony
Description: A colony established by the Galactic Empire existed on the planet of Herios IV during the Galactic Civil War, a conflict between the Empire and the Rebel Alliance. The colony was overseen by an Imperial governor.[1]

Name: Unidentified mining colony (Weldii III)
Description: A mining colony was located on[1] the Core[2] world of Weldii III.[1]

Name: Unidentified Tarnab colony
Description: The colony was mentioned in Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy, a 2016 reference book written by Pablo Hidalgo.[1]

Name: Unidentified tree mountain colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified tree ocean colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified Twi'lek colony
Description: The title of this article is conjectural.

Name: Unidentified underground mining colony
Description: An underground mining colony was located on an airless moon in the Outer Rim Territories. Most of its residents had never seen stars. Ryland and Fiona used to work there, and their daughter Laina was born there.[1]

Name: Unidentified Viidaav colony
Description: The Viidaavs founded a mining colony on the surface of their homeworld, Viidaav. During the Clone Wars, the Confederacy of Independent Systems bought ore mined in the colony.

Name: Unidentified Wookiee base (Attichitcuk's rescue)
Description: Wookiee colonists constructed a base on the moon Alaris Prime between 39 BBY and 36 BBY as part of a colonial war fought against the Trade Federation. It was established for the purpose of training bowcaster troopers to combat the Trade Federation Droid Army. The base also had access to multiple sources of food, which were harvested to support the Wookiee forces. When the colony's leader, Attichitcuk, was badly wound

Name: Unidentified Wookiee base (fortress)
Description: A base was built by Wookiee colonists on the moon Alaris Prime between 39 and 36 BBY. It was constructed south of a base belonging to the Trade Federation, an organization the Wookiees were at war with. The buildings inside the base were constructed and used by Wookiee workers, while parts of its outer walls were constructed by the Wookiee Shoran. At the base, the leader of the colonists, Chewbacca, planned an assaul

Name: Unidentified Wookiee base (shipyard)
Description: A base was built by Wookiee colonists on the moon Alaris Prime close in proximity to a Trade Federation outpost. The base had access to several vital resources, including food, carbon, nova crystals, and ore. The layout of the base was initially composed of a command center, three prefab shelters, and an animal nursery, and was expanded to include several resource storage and military buildings. One of these building

Name: Vactooine mining colony
Description: The Vactooine mining colony was a mining colony on the planet Vactooine that was wiped out by Gamorrean raiders during the Cold War between the Galactic Republic and the Sith Empire.[1]

Name: Waskiro colony
Description: The Waskiro colony was a colony settlement of the Galactic Republic on the planet Waskiro. During the Cold War with the Sith Empire an attack on Waskiro was launched by combat droids of an unknown make. The few survivors were unable to identify the attackers' allegiance when the Republic came to investigate why the colony had gone silent.[1]

Name: Wetyin's Colony
Description: Wetyin's Colony was the main settlement on the moon Yavin 4 that was established when settlers traveled from the planet Setor to become farmers.[3] In the months after the Battle of Endor, Shara Bey and Kes Dameron settled in a new home in the colony after completing their service in the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Having their own home had been their dream after being separated from one another, as well as the

Name: Yarkora colony
Description: The Yarkora colony was mentioned in Pablo Hidalgo's 2025 reference book Star Wars: The Acolyte: The Visual Guide.[1]

Name: Canyon Oasis
Description: Canyon Oasis first appeared in the video game Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II as a multiplayer level for deathmatch, referred to in game as Jedi Training, a game mode where players fought against other players by themselves. Although the map's precise location is unclear, the textures used in the map are also used in singleplayer levels depicting the planet Ruusan. The multiplayer mode could allow up to eight

Name: Derven Acos
Description: Derven Acos was first mentioned during the during the 2026 Summer Game Fest, it was mentioned in an announcement immediately before the Story Trailer. Although this announcement called Derven Acos a planet,[3] later sources clarified the spelling[1] and confirmed it is actually an arena located on a ringed celestial body.[2][4]

Name: Droids' Paradise
Description: Droids' Paradise was a rumored location. After Order 66, Jedi Knight[1] Nagi Tsukumo[2] accompanied a pair of droids to the Droids' Paradise while being pursued by the Galactic Empire.[1]

Name: Madar
Description: During the Great Jedi Purge, a last stand of Jedi survivors fought Republic forces at a mountain fortress. Grand Admiral Rae Sloane vaguely recalled that the fortress was on Madar or Morad.[1]

Name: Morad
Description: During the Great Jedi Purge, a last stand of Jedi survivors fought Republic forces at a mountain fortress. Grand Admiral Rae Sloane vaguely recalled that the fortress was on Morad or Madar.[1]

Name: Pits of Paxxis
Description: The Pits of Paxxis were a location at which, according to Sith legend, the Dreamer and the Monster-Slayer dueled.[1]

Name: Qesh
Description: Qesh was a location[2] with a scrapyard on it. The scavenger droid P3-KTO originated in the scrapyard on Qesh, which was mentioned during commentary by host Dorzek Senn[1] and commentator Haz Bakkel at the Droid Destruction Circuit in the settlement Galma on the planet Batuu when the droid fought there[1] in 34 ABY.[3]

Description: The "Last Word" Launch/Targeter System was first mentioned in No Disintegrations,[1] a 2017 supplement for the Star Wars: Edge of the Empire roleplaying game published by Fantasy Flight Games.[2]

Description: "Pleasure planet" sauce was a type of sauce. It was used in Zeltros sliders served at Dex's Diner on the planet of Coruscant.[1]

Name: "Sole Survivor" Backup Computer Core
Description: The "Sole Survivor" Backup Computer Core was first mentioned in Fully Operational,[1] a 2018 sourcebook for the Star Wars: Age of Rebellion roleplaying game published by Fantasy Flight Games.[2]

Name: "Surefire" Enhancement System
Description: The "Surefire" Enhancement System was first mentioned in Fully Operational,[1] a 2018 sourcebook for the Star Wars: Age of Rebellion roleplaying game published by Fantasy Flight Games.[2]

Name: "Worldsplitter" Plasma Drill
Description: The "Worldsplitter" Plasma Drill was first mentioned in Fully Operational,[1] a 2018 sourcebook for the Star Wars: Age of Rebellion roleplaying game published by Fantasy Flight Games.[2]

Name: 'ABY-BBY' dating system
Description: The 'ABY-BBY' dating system was a chronological framework used to differentiate years before the Battle of Yavin (designated as BBY) from those following it (ABY). As one of multiple dating conventions employed throughout the galaxy, it provided a structured means of historical reference within various records and scholarly works.

The population was seen as rather xenophobic compared to most worlds of the Republic. The star system had its own currency.

The ambush resulted in Nord and his henchmen's deaths.

Darth Vader and the 501st Legion were ambushed during their search for Admiral Garoche Tarkin. In actuality, he had defected to Lady Saro, High Priestess of Atoa, one of the rulers of the system. Saro and Tarkin were eventually killed by Vader on the planet.

Description: A planet located in the Atoan system, in the Ghost Nebula, was covered by tar pits generating oceans of boiling tar. It was inhabited by Atoans from the neighboring planet of Atoa.

Name: Atollon system

Name: Battle of the Rettna system
Description: A battle occurred after negotiations about disrupted mining rights in the Rettna system between Freda and Jante collapsed. The Freda system militias launched a surprise attack on Jante holdings; the 1st, 3rd and 5th Lon Flotillas launched the assault, destroying the Hamner Heavy Ores Plant in orbit of Rett II, and striking two installations belonging to the Jante Materials Corporation.

Name: Battle of the Rial Kroon system
Description: In 22 BBY, during the Clone Wars, a battle was fought in the Rial Kroon system. Jedi Master Toluz Cheq's Republic fleet was decimated by a new model of Confederate Vulture droids equipped with advanced engines constructed by famous starship builders from the planet Valahari. Following the battle, the Jedi attempted to convince the Valahari to stop supplying the Confederacy with the engines but failed to do so.
